twitter_thread: Daron Acemoglu claims 70% of AI investments are wasted on automation that doesn't increase productivity, citing a $100bn annual loss.
Acemoglu's research shows AI adoption in manufacturing leads to 25% job displacement, with no significant productivity gains.
Using AI to cut 10% of labor costs sounds good, but it often means cutting 20% of skilled jobs, with no innovation to show for it.
A key mistake is using chatbots to replace human customer support, rather than using AI to analyze and improve support processes.
Acemoglu recommends focusing on AI-driven process improvements, like predictive maintenance, which can increase productivity by 15%.
What's your experience with AI adoption in your industry: has it driven growth or just cut costs?
Has AI increased productivity in your workplace, or just replaced 5-10 jobs? #aiadoption #productivity
pack.linkedin: Nobel laureate Daron Acemoglu's research reveals that $100bn in AI investments is wasted annually on automation that doesn't increase productivity. He argues that AI adoption in manufacturing leads to 25% job displacement, with no significant productivity gains. Acemoglu recommends focusing on AI-driven process improvements, like predictive maintenance, which can increase productivity by 15%. What's your experience with AI adoption in your industry: has it driven growth or just cut 10-20% of labor costs?
tiktok_hooks: One hundred billion dollars in AI investments are going down the drain every year
AI is replacing skilled jobs at twice the rate of unskilled jobs
The AI adoption trap: cutting labor costs by 10%, but losing 20% of your innovation potential
pack.reddit_headline: Daron Acemoglu claims 70% of AI investments are wasted: can you name one successful AI-driven process improvement in your industry?
